nodes.title: 25.1 MERIT SCHOLARSHIP – BE/BTech STUDENTS
nodes.summary: This document outlines merit scholarships for BE/BTech students, detailing criteria for first-year admission based on JEE Mains-2026 AIR and 10+2 marks, as well as continuing eligibility from the second year onwards based on CGPA and academic performance. Specific scholarships include MERIT SCHOLARSHIPS-I, II, and III, with varying percentage requirements and benefits like tuition and development fee waivers. It also details MERIT SCHOLARSHIP IV (A), (B), and (C) for Biotechnology and Biomedical Engineering students, with criteria based on JEE AIR and Institute rank using PCB marks. Additionally, MERIT SCHOLARSHIPS-VI (A) is specified for lateral entry students based on diploma marks.
nodes.text:

nodes.title: 25.2 MERIT-CUM-MEANS SCHOLARSHIPS FOR BE/BTech STUDENTS :
nodes.summary: The document outlines Merit-cum-Means Scholarships for BE/BTech students, categorized into Normal, Special, and Lateral Entry. All scholarships have a gross family income ceiling of ₹10.00 lac. The Normal scholarships are awarded based on income for the first year and CGPA of 7.50 with no backlog from the second year. Special scholarships are for deserving cases not covered by the Normal category. Lateral Entry scholarships have specific criteria for diploma marks and income for initial eligibility, transitioning to CGPA and income requirements from the second year. Preference is given to girl students from families with no male child in case of income ties. Scholarship finalization is handled by a committee of Deans and the Registrar. The value of all these scholarships is a waiver of Tuition and Development Fees. Lateral entry students are considered separately and can apply with other students from the third year onwards.

nodes.title: 25.3 SCHOLARSHIP – ME/M.Tech. STUDENTS
nodes.summary: This text outlines the scholarship provisions for ME/M.Tech. students. Scholarships are available from AICTE for GATE-qualified candidates, subject to approval and funding. TIET offers an additional Rs. 5000/- per month to those receiving AICTE or other funding. GATE-qualified students not receiving external funding are eligible for Rs. 12,400/- per month from TIET. Non-GATE qualified students receive Rs. 10,000/- per month for 10 months, with minimum qualifying degree marks required (70% general, 65% SC/ST). Scholarship continuation in the second year necessitates an 8.00 CGPA and no backlog from the first year.
nodes.text:
## 25.3 SCHOLARSHIP – ME/M.Tech. STUDENTS

The candidates admitted in M.E./M.Tech. will be eligible for the scholarship as per the following:

I. **Scholarship from AICTE**: -
Candidates with valid GATE score, will be considered to receive scholarships only if approved and amount released by AICTE or any other funding agency (applicable only for intake approved by AICTE).

II. **Scholarship from TIET**: -
Candidates with valid GATE score and who are getting scholarship from AICTE or from any other funding agency as mentioned at S.No. (I) above, shall also be eligible for **additional scholarship** of Rs. 5000/- per month from TIET.

Candidates with valid GATE score and who are not getting scholarship from AICTE or from any other funding agency as mentioned at S.No. (I) above, shall be offered a scholarship of Rs. 12,400/- per month by TIET.

ME/MTech Candidates who are not GATE qualified, shall be offered Rs. 10,000/- per month for 10 months in an academic year. A minimum 70% (65% for SC/ST) of marks in qualifying degree will be required to avail these scholarships. For continuation of Scholarship in second year, minimum 8.00 CGPA at the end of first year will be required with no backlog.

nodes.title: 25.4 SCHOLARSHIP – MA STUDENTS
nodes.summary: This text outlines the scholarship criteria for MA students, detailing two merit-based scholarships (A and B) with varying aggregate mark requirements at the graduation level. Both scholarships require a minimum CGPA of 8.50 in preceding semesters, no backlogs, and successful completion of all prior courses. Scholarship A offers 50% of the Tuition Fee for students with 75% or more aggregate marks, while Scholarship B offers 30% for those with 65% to less than 75% aggregate marks. Merit scholarships are contingent upon a minimum of five students being registered in the respective discipline.
nodes.text:
## 25.4 SCHOLARSHIP – MA STUDENTS

The candidates admitted in MA will be eligible for the scholarship as per the following:

|  MERIT SCHOLARSHIPS-MA (A) | Aggregate Marks ≥ 75% at qualifying exam (Graduation Level) | All eligible students | 50% of TF | Minimum 8.50 CGPA in all the preceding semester(s) and should not have any backlog in the year of consideration and must have passed all the courses offered in the semester(s) prior to the year of consideration.  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  MERIT SCHOLARSHIPS-MA (B) | Aggregate Marks ≥ 65% But < 75% at qualifying exam (Graduation Level) | All eligible students | 30% of TF | Minimum 8.50 CGPA in all the preceding semester(s) and should not have any backlog in the year of consideration and must have passed all the courses offered in the semester(s) prior to the year of consideration.  |

Merit scholarships will be awarded only if the number of students registered in a discipline is at least five.

nodes.title: 25.5 SCHOLARSHIP – MCA STUDENTS
nodes.summary:
## 25.5 SCHOLARSHIP – MCA STUDENTS

The scholarship (10% of admitted strength) will be awarded in the first year to MCA students (in second semester) on the basis of performance (CGPA obtained) in the first semester. The scholarship will be awarded in the succeeding year on the basis of merit i.e. AGPA of the preceding year. The eligible students should not have an AGPA less than 8 and should not have any backlog in the year of consideration and must have passed all the courses offered in the semester(s) prior to the year of consideration.

The scholarship will be Rs. 40,000 p.a.

nodes.title: 25.6 ELIGIBILITY
nodes.summary: This document outlines the eligibility criteria for Institute merit scholarships. Key points include the requirement to clear all program courses, specific award criteria for B.E./B.Tech. scholarships based on AGPA and other factors, and conditions for students who are upgraded, are foreign/NRI/IEP students, or receive external financial aid. It also clarifies that there are no income limits for parents, students can only receive one scholarship if eligible for multiple, and scholarships are contingent on minimum student enrollment in a discipline. Special provisions are made for IEP students regarding branch matching and returning to normal courses, including the obligation to repay scholarships under certain circumstances.
nodes.text:
## 25.6 ELIGIBILITY

I. Only those students will be eligible for the award of Institute merit scholarships who have cleared all the courses prescribed for the respective programme in the year of consideration.
II. Merit Scholarships-I, Merit Scholarships-II, Merit Scholarships-IV(A), Merit Scholarships-IV(B) of B.E./B.Tech. shall be awarded to the students as per prescribed criteria in the above table.
III. Merit Scholarships-III, Merit Scholarships-IV(C) covering 10% of the students' strength (other than over and above intake) of B.E./B.Tech. shall be awarded every year on the basis of AGPA earned in the preceding year and criteria mentioned in above table.
IV. In case student is upgraded through December Test in 1st year, he/she will not be eligible for merit scholarship in 2nd year on the basis of his/her performance in 1st year.
V. In case FN/NRI student or IEP student is selected for merit scholarship-III in second year or subsequent years, he/she will be given award amount as per the tuition fee charged from Indian students of the same batch.
VI. The student who has been awarded merit scholarship by the Institute are entitled to receive stipend or any financial assistance from any other source. However, if the student is awarded

any stipend or financial assistance from any other source, he shall immediately communicate the same to Dean of Academic Affairs (DoAA).

VII. There will be no income limit of the guardians/parents of the students for the grant of merit scholarships.
VIII. In case a student is eligible for more than one scholarship/free ship, he/she shall be awarded only one scholarship/free ship as per his/her preference.
IX. Merit scholarships will be awarded only if the number of students registered in a discipline/program is at least five.
X. IEP students whose parent branch is different than the IEP branch, will not be considered for TIET Merit Scholarship. Only those cases will be considered for Merit scholarship whose parent branch and IEP branch is same. In case a student return to his normal course from IEP course, he may be given benefit of scholarship, provided the student will submit an undertaking that if he again goes to IEP Course, he will return the entire scholarship benefit to the institute. Also the student will not claim scholarship of previous semester's when he / she were IEP student.
XI. In case any normal student who has been awarded scholarship in 1st and 2nd year joins the IEP course later on, he/ she will have to return the entire scholarship amount back to institute.

nodes.title: 25.7 SELECTION
nodes.summary:
## 25.7 SELECTION

I. The award of merit scholarships for B.E./ B.Tech., LEET, MCA and M.Sc. programs shall be announced every year by the DoAA.
II. If the student declines (or is not awarded merit scholarship for certain reasons) it shall be awarded to the next student in the approved list.
III. The merit scholarships shall be tenable for one academic year.

nodes.title: 25.8 TIE BREAKER FOR CONTINUATION OF SCHOLARSHIP
nodes.summary:
## 25.8 TIE BREAKER FOR CONTINUATION OF SCHOLARSHIP

In case of a tie at AGPA, it shall be broken by considering number of A+ grades secured by the students during the period under consideration for calculation of AGPA. If number of A+ grades secured by students are same then tie shall be broken by considering the students' performance on the basis of CGPA. If the CGPA is also same, the tie shall be broken using the percentage of marks obtained by the students in all the courses of last two semesters. If, however, the tie still exists then it will be broken by considering the merit of the entrance examination taken at entry level.

If, however, the tie still exists then it will be broken by considering the merit of the entrance examination taken at entry level.

nodes.title: 25.9 WITHDRAWAL
nodes.summary:
## 25.9 WITHDRAWAL

If a recipient of merit scholarship is found guilty of gross misconduct his/her case after due warning shall be reported by the DoAA for withdrawal of merit scholarship and decision in this regard will be taken by the Pro Vice Chancellor.

nodes.title: 25.10 OTHER SCHOLARSHIPS/STIPENDS
nodes.summary: The document details a comprehensive list of scholarships and stipends available to students, categorized by their nature and eligibility criteria. These include merit-cum-means scholarships, scholarships for specific demographic groups (girl students, SC/ST, disabled, orphans, minorities, J&K students), scholarships based on academic merit (CGPA, AGPA, entrance rank), and scholarships instituted by alumni and charitable foundations. Various government schemes like Central Sector Scholarships, AICTE scholarships, and state-specific scholarships are outlined, along with their respective income ceilings, academic requirements, and application procedures. Specific instructions are provided for applying to government and Punjab government scholarships, emphasizing online registration and document submission. Several scholarships are named after individuals or alumni batches, with varying award amounts and specific eligibility conditions related to academic performance, financial need, and sometimes extracurricular achievements. A note also highlights the provision for awarding scholarships to female engineering students.

nodes.title: Notes:
nodes.summary: This document outlines scholarship eligibility and application procedures. It states that students admitted/upgraded in the December Test are ineligible, while Government of India nominated students and those sponsored by industrial houses are eligible. Any changes to Government scholarship amounts or eligibility will follow the latest circular. For direct transfer of Government scholarship funds, Aadhaar-enabled bank accounts are required. Eligible SC/ST, OBC, and PwD students must submit their applications and supporting documents to the Scholarship Section in DOSA after online application, adhering to the deadlines posted on government agency web portals. Students are limited to applying for one scholarship. Additionally, biometric attendance is mandatory for reserve category students receiving scholarships from government agencies.
nodes.text:
## Notes:

I. No scholarship shall be offered to students admitted/upgraded in December Test.

II. Students nominated by the Government of India are also awarded Scholarship by their respective Governments.

III. Sponsored Industrial House Scholarhisp would also be available.

IV. In case of any change regarding amount or eligibility for the Government scholarships mentioned in above table, the latest circular shall be applicable.

V. For availing Government scholarships, Aadhaar enabled bank account or linking of existing bank account with Aadhaar number is required at the time of applying of scholarship for direct transfer of scholarship amount to the beneficiary, as per the guidelines issued by the Government from time to time.

VI. All the eligible SC/ST, OBC, PwD students etc. should submit their scholarship forms along with all other relevant documents in the Scholarship Section in DOSA office, after applying online at respective web portals for the academic year on or before the last date of submission of application form. The last date of the scholarships will be displayed on the respective web portal of the concerned scholarship schemes by the Govt. agencies. Student can apply for one scholarship only.

VII. As per the guidelines of the Government agencies, attendance through Biometric system is mandatory for reserve category students who are eligible for scholarships from these agencies. The location of the Biometric system for taking the attendance will be displayed on the notice boards of the Institute.

SCHOLARSHIPS- POST GRADUATE STUDENTS

## 25.3 SCHOLARSHIP – ME/M.Tech. STUDENTS

The candidates admitted in M.E./M.Tech. will be eligible for the scholarship as per the following:

I. **Scholarship from AICTE**: -
Candidates with valid GATE score, will be considered to receive scholarships only if approved and amount released by AICTE or any other funding agency (applicable only for intake approved by AICTE).

II. **Scholarship from TIET**: -
Candidates with valid GATE score and who are getting scholarship from AICTE or from any other funding agency as mentioned at S.No. (I) above, shall also be eligible for **additional scholarship** of Rs. 5000/- per month from TIET.

Candidates with valid GATE score and who are not getting scholarship from AICTE or from any other funding agency as mentioned at S.No. (I) above, shall be offered a scholarship of Rs. 12,400/- per month by TIET.

ME/MTech Candidates who are not GATE qualified, shall be offered Rs. 10,000/- per month for 10 months in an academic year. A minimum 70% (65% for SC/ST) of marks in qualifying degree will be required to avail these scholarships. For continuation of Scholarship in second year, minimum 8.00 CGPA at the end of first year will be required with no backlog.

## 25.4 SCHOLARSHIP – MA STUDENTS

The candidates admitted in MA will be eligible for the scholarship as per the following:

|  MERIT SCHOLARSHIPS-MA (A) | Aggregate Marks ≥ 75% at qualifying exam (Graduation Level) | All eligible students | 50% of TF | Minimum 8.50 CGPA in all the preceding semester(s) and should not have any backlog in the year of consideration and must have passed all the courses offered in the semester(s) prior to the year of consideration.  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  MERIT SCHOLARSHIPS-MA (B) | Aggregate Marks ≥ 65% But < 75% at qualifying exam (Graduation Level) | All eligible students | 30% of TF | Minimum 8.50 CGPA in all the preceding semester(s) and should not have any backlog in the year of consideration and must have passed all the courses offered in the semester(s) prior to the year of consideration.  |

Merit scholarships will be awarded only if the number of students registered in a discipline is at least five.

## 25.5 SCHOLARSHIP – MCA STUDENTS

The scholarship (10% of admitted strength) will be awarded in the first year to MCA students (in second semester) on the basis of performance (CGPA obtained) in the first semester. The scholarship will be awarded in the succeeding year on the basis of merit i.e. AGPA of the preceding year. The eligible students should not have an AGPA less than 8 and should not have any backlog in the year of consideration and must have passed all the courses offered in the semester(s) prior to the year of consideration.

The scholarship will be Rs. 40,000 p.a.

## 25.6 ELIGIBILITY

I. Only those students will be eligible for the award of Institute merit scholarships who have cleared all the courses prescribed for the respective programme in the year of consideration.
II. Merit Scholarships-I, Merit Scholarships-II, Merit Scholarships-IV(A), Merit Scholarships-IV(B) of B.E./B.Tech. shall be awarded to the students as per prescribed criteria in the above table.
III. Merit Scholarships-III, Merit Scholarships-IV(C) covering 10% of the students' strength (other than over and above intake) of B.E./B.Tech. shall be awarded every year on the basis of AGPA earned in the preceding year and criteria mentioned in above table.
IV. In case student is upgraded through December Test in 1st year, he/she will not be eligible for merit scholarship in 2nd year on the basis of his/her performance in 1st year.
V. In case FN/NRI student or IEP student is selected for merit scholarship-III in second year or subsequent years, he/she will be given award amount as per the tuition fee charged from Indian students of the same batch.
VI. The student who has been awarded merit scholarship by the Institute are entitled to receive stipend or any financial assistance from any other source. However, if the student is awarded

any stipend or financial assistance from any other source, he shall immediately communicate the same to Dean of Academic Affairs (DoAA).

VII. There will be no income limit of the guardians/parents of the students for the grant of merit scholarships.
VIII. In case a student is eligible for more than one scholarship/free ship, he/she shall be awarded only one scholarship/free ship as per his/her preference.
IX. Merit scholarships will be awarded only if the number of students registered in a discipline/program is at least five.
X. IEP students whose parent branch is different than the IEP branch, will not be considered for TIET Merit Scholarship. Only those cases will be considered for Merit scholarship whose parent branch and IEP branch is same. In case a student return to his normal course from IEP course, he may be given benefit of scholarship, provided the student will submit an undertaking that if he again goes to IEP Course, he will return the entire scholarship benefit to the institute. Also the student will not claim scholarship of previous semester's when he / she were IEP student.
XI. In case any normal student who has been awarded scholarship in 1st and 2nd year joins the IEP course later on, he/ she will have to return the entire scholarship amount back to institute.

## 25.7 SELECTION

I. The award of merit scholarships for B.E./ B.Tech., LEET, MCA and M.Sc. programs shall be announced every year by the DoAA.
II. If the student declines (or is not awarded merit scholarship for certain reasons) it shall be awarded to the next student in the approved list.
III. The merit scholarships shall be tenable for one academic year.

## 25.8 TIE BREAKER FOR CONTINUATION OF SCHOLARSHIP

In case of a tie at AGPA, it shall be broken by considering number of A+ grades secured by the students during the period under consideration for calculation of AGPA. If number of A+ grades secured by students are same then tie shall be broken by considering the students' performance on the basis of CGPA. If the CGPA is also same, the tie shall be broken using the percentage of marks obtained by the students in all the courses of last two semesters. If, however, the tie still exists then it will be broken by considering the merit of the entrance examination taken at entry level.

## 25.9 WITHDRAWAL

If a recipient of merit scholarship is found guilty of gross misconduct his/her case after due warning shall be reported by the DoAA for withdrawal of merit scholarship and decision in this regard will be taken by the Pro Vice Chancellor.

## Notes:

I. No scholarship shall be offered to students admitted/upgraded in December Test.

II. Students nominated by the Government of India are also awarded Scholarship by their respective Governments.

III. Sponsored Industrial House Scholarhisp would also be available.

IV. In case of any change regarding amount or eligibility for the Government scholarships mentioned in above table, the latest circular shall be applicable.

V. For availing Government scholarships, Aadhaar enabled bank account or linking of existing bank account with Aadhaar number is required at the time of applying of scholarship for direct transfer of scholarship amount to the beneficiary, as per the guidelines issued by the Government from time to time.

VI. All the eligible SC/ST, OBC, PwD students etc. should submit their scholarship forms along with all other relevant documents in the Scholarship Section in DOSA office, after applying online at respective web portals for the academic year on or before the last date of submission of application form. The last date of the scholarships will be displayed on the respective web portal of the concerned scholarship schemes by the Govt. agencies. Student can apply for one scholarship only.

VII. As per the guidelines of the Government agencies, attendance through Biometric system is mandatory for reserve category students who are eligible for scholarships from these agencies. The location of the Biometric system for taking the attendance will be displayed on the notice boards of the Institute.
